items.title: What changed in Laravel 11 routing
items.excerpt: A quick look at bootstrap/app.php and how route registration moved away from Kernel.php.
items.author: Shahadat Hossain

content.text: Laravel 11 simplified the application skeleton significantly. Route registration, middleware, and exception handling all moved into bootstrap/app.php, replacing the old Kernel.php files most Laravel developers grew up with.

By default, a fresh Laravel 11 install only registers routes/web.php. If your project needs an API, you have to explicitly tell withRouting() where to find routes/api.php — otherwise every API route silently 404s, which is a confusing first bug to hit.
Once you register it explicitly, everything works the same way you'd expect from Laravel 10 — it's really just a relocation of configuration, not a change in behavior.
Overall, the change makes small projects easier to reason about, at the cost of a few gotchas for anyone porting an older app forward.

items.title: Why pinning reanimated versions matters
items.excerpt: A note on keeping react-native-reanimated and worklets versions in sync for Expo Go.
items.author: Shahadat Hossain

content.text: When working with Expo SDK 54, pinning react-native-reanimated@4.1.1 and react-native-worklets@0.5.1 avoids native crash issues that show up only on physical devices, not in the simulator.

content.caption: Version mismatches only show up at runtime

content.text: The safest approach is to lock exact versions in package.json rather than using caret ranges, then verify with expo doctor before every release build.
